entry.title: Washington Post: 20 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Turkey meets NATO hopefuls Sweden, Finland; Ukraine ramps up counterattacks
  • In major blow, Macron loses absolute majority in French Parliament
  • Spain devastated by wildfires amid record-breaking heat wave
  • Five British men released from Taliban custody
  • Last month, China imported more Russian crude oil than ever before
  • Ukraine awaits E.U. candidate decision; leaders prepare for long war
  • Europe rallies behind Ukraine. But fatigue is around the corner.
  • Gustavo Petro, former guerrilla, will be Colombia’s first leftist president
  • Hundreds of civilians missing, taken or simply gone: The untold toll of the Ukraine war
  • Zelensky in Father’s Day message praises dads who defend Ukraine
  • Cambodian catches world's largest recorded freshwater fish
  • Israel says regional alliance has thwarted Iranian attacks
  • William at 40: A milestone birthday in a life under scrutiny
  • Denmark charges 3 with planning to carry out terror attack
  • Canada sets dates to ban some single-use plastics
  • Colombia picks 1st leftist president in tight runoff contest
  • Suspected jihadi rebels kill 40 civilians in central Mali
  • Officials try to deliver aid to flooded South Asia villages
  • Festival founded by Dua Lipa's father returns to Kosovo
  • China's 1st Horn of Africa envoy offers to mediate in region
  • Investors paid thousands for rare wines. It was a scam, feds say.
  • Held at gunpoint behind the wheel, a driver sped to alert officers
  • Louisville mayor punched by man in second attack on local Democrats
  • Floridians give DeSantis points for his covid stance. Will it hold?
  • Why this teacher keeps one chair empty in his middle school classroom
  • This Texas teen wanted an abortion. She now has twins.
  • Bats and balls — but not baseball — win over Texas kids
  • Biden’s ‘energy whisperer’ balances climate agenda, rising gas prices
  • Oil refineries are making a windfall. Why do they keep closing?
  • Why gas is so expensive in some U.S. states but not others
  • A picture-perfect metaverse is years away. Meta’s prototypes prove it.
  • Amazon drones are coming to town. Some locals want to shoot them.
  • How to hire a reliable roofing contractor for your home
  • Association requiring owner to upgrade roof to match aesthetic changes
  • Two-bedroom condo on Connecticut Avenue NW lists for $350,000
  • NATO Must Bring Finland, Sweden and Turkey Together
  • The West Is Facing a Followership Crisis
  • Juneteenth Is a Day for Cheer — and Unfinished Business
  • The Weakness of Putin’s Economic Show of Force
  • How an Emboldened Far-Right Is Changing French Politics
  • Why the ECB Needs New Tools for Bond ‘Fragmentation’
  • How Europe Became So Dependent on Putin for Its Gas
  • How to Access the UK Cost-of-Living Support Package
  • How the GOP is making national policy one state at a time
  • A generation of homeowners encounters a strange new market
  • This isn’t just Ukraine’s war. It’s our war too. Act accordingly.
  • Be careful about lionizing Jan. 6 witnesses. They failed to stop the insurrection.
  • Finally, ‘Top Gun’ has a female pilot. Real life should catch up.
  • As an adoptee, I know: Adoption is not a fairy-tale answer to abortion
  • The GOP’s inconsistencies, contradictions and disconnects
  • No point of view should enjoy greater legal protection than other perspectives
  • Personal financial training is great, but it’s not enough
  • Undermining democratic elections in Loudoun County
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Guardian: 20 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Taliban release five British nationals held for six months
  • Heathrow asks airlines to cancel 10% of flights today, as easyJet cuts summer schedule – business live
  • Russia-Ukraine war: Russian blockade of grain exports is ‘a real war crime’, says EU foreign policy chief – live
  • The rail strikes are just the start. Tory cruelty has left workers no choice but to fight back | Polly Toynbee
  • Gustavo Petro: first leftist president faces tough challenge in Colombia
  • Football quiz: players making the move south from Scotland to England
  • Elvis Presley as Willy Wonka? The films the King of Rock’n’Roll almost made
  • Russia condemns Lithuania transit ban on some goods to Kaliningrad
  • Boris Johnson admitted to hospital for routine surgery, leaving Raab in charge
  • ‘It’s death either way’: desperate Tigrayans flee starvation and forced conscription
  • Russian emails appear to show ‘network’ holding $4.5bn assets linked to Putin
  • Culture matters around the world. What a shame it has been toxically weaponised in the UK | Charlotte Higgins
  • Crimes against civilians: documenting the scale of abuse in Ukraine
  • Alanis Morissette review – Jagged Little Pill still has sharp edges
  • Unfinished and unwanted 9,000-passenger cruise ship to be scrapped
  • Macron has been sent tumbling to Earth – now he’ll have to learn to compromise | Mujtaba Rahman
  • ‘It’s a middle-class dream come true!’ How the utility room became the new status symbol
  • French PM under pressure after Macron’s alliance loses absolute majority
  • Threat of Fifa ban looms over India and Chhetri’s hopes of passing Messi | John Duerden
  • How we met: ‘My family wanted an arranged marriage, but I couldn’t bear the idea’
  • Moon, 66 Questions review – elusive but rewarding study of family tension
  • Rachel Roddy’s recipe for deep-fried little fish and courgette matchsticks | A kitchen in Rome
  • Thomasina Miers’ recipe for spiced turmeric chicken bake with golden potatoes | The new flexitarian
  • Belgium returns Patrice Lumumba’s tooth to family 61 years after his murder
  • Billy Vunipola recalled to solve No 8 problem for England’s tour of Australia
  • ‘Children ask why I’ve got a wonky face’: life with Justin Bieber’s Ramsay Hunt syndrome
  • ‘Fluffy’ crab that wears a sponge as a hat discovered in Western Australia
  • Sudanese museums seek return of artefacts taken by British colonisers
  • Unhoused in Phoenix’s perilous heat: ‘If I don’t keep cool, I’ll die’
  • Someone has to hold the government accountable (unless it is too soon). Introducing: ALBO WATCH | First Dog on the Moon
  • ‘I’m appalled’: The artist who put up a giant sign for refugees at sea to read
  • Appalling abuse in British Gymnastics shows urgent need for sports regulator | Sean Ingle
  • ‘It’s like a rock opera’: Converge’s Jacob Bannon and Chelsea Wolfe stir up beautiful metal
  • Liz Cheney’s condemnation of Trump’s lies wins over Democrats
  • ‘We just pray for rain’: Niger is in the eye of the climate crisis – and children are starving
  • A local’s guide to Hamburg: concerts, cocktails and dockyard cuisine
  • Planning for the long haul: four months on, the Ukrainian refugee crisis is only just beginning
  • MDMA trials under review in Canada over alleged abuse of study participants
  • ‘I wish I’d written Redemption Song’: Stephen Marley’s honest playlist
  • A geeky brown girl in a big white world? Ms Marvel is my kind of superhero | Saima Mir
  • A new start after 60: ‘I became a vegan at 82 and found a new sense of freedom’
  • Russia becomes China’s top oil supplier amid Ukraine war sanctions
  • Rise of the sides: how Grindr finally recognized gay men who aren’t tops or bottoms
  • The forgotten ‘weird sisters’ of WB Yeats who helped forge Irish identity
  • Only a tiny minority of rural Britons are farmers – so why do they hold such sway? | George Monbiot
  • ‘Images you can smell’ – novelist Orhan Pamuk on Dayanita Singh’s mesmerising photos of India’s disintegrating archives
  • US calls on Vietnam to release environmental activist Nguy Thi Khanh
  • China carries out anti-missile tests amid opposition to US systems in South Korea
  • Former guerrilla Gustavo Petro wins Colombian election to become first leftist president
  • Matt Fitzpatrick sets sights on six majors after opening account with US Open win
  • From New Zealand to Nigeria: the global toll of the cost of living crisis
  • Spirals of blue light in New Zealand night sky leave stargazers ‘kind of freaking out’
  • Ukraine restricts Russian books and music in latest step of ‘derussification’
  • Spain: conservative People’s party wins unprecedented majority in Andalucía
  • Max Verstappen holds off Carlos Sainz to win Canadian Grand Prix
  • From Vanuatu law school to the Hague: the fight to recognise climate harm in international law
  • Film-maker Paul Haggis arrested over sexual assault allegations in Italy
  • Jones insists he can take England forward despite Barbarians humiliation
  • Transgender women swimmers barred from female competitions by Fina
  • Spain battles wildfires fuelled by one of earliest heatwaves on record
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

entry.title: NY Times: 20 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Covid Cases Surge, but Deaths Stay Near Lows
  • Surviving Russia’s ‘Filtration Camps’
  • Russia becomes China’s biggest crude-oil supplier as Europe cuts imports.
  • With Roe Set to End, Many Women Worry About High-Risk Pregnancies
  • Inside a Florida Abortion Clinic Ahead of the Supreme Court’s Decision
  • The Supreme Court, Public Opinion and the Fate of Roe
  • Gustavo Petro Wins the Election, Becoming Colombia’s First Leftist Leader
  • Gustavo Petro derrota a Rodolfo Hernández en Colombia

  • Texas Republicans Approve Far-Right Platform Declaring Biden’s Election Illegitimate
  • Red Flags for Forced Labor Found in China’s Car Battery Supply Chain
  • Autopsies Have a History of Costly Mistakes, Yet Change Is Slow
  • The Incredible Journey of Three African Wild Dogs
  • Mike Pence Was of Two Minds
  • Prosecute Trump? Put Yourself in Merrick Garland’s Shoes
  • How the Abortion and Gun Debates Look From Alabama
  • Are We Sure America Is Not at War in Ukraine?
  • Feminism Made a Faustian Bargain With Celebrity Culture. Now It’s Paying the Price.
  • How Animals See Themselves
  • Let’s Pass the Recovering America’s Wildlife Act
  • A Wildfire Year
  • NFL Players Participate in a Study That Might Predict CTE Decline
  • In NY Lieutenant Governor Race, Delgado Faces Sharp Challenge From Archila
  • Tattoo Artists Face a Grayer Palette in Europe
  • Ukraine Live Updates: Eastern Town Is Flash Point in Battle for Donbas
  • New Brand of Activist Takes Aim at Ukraine War and Climate Crisis, Together
  • Over 200 Feared Dead in Ethiopia Massacre
  • Preserving the Music of Morocco’s Sephardic Jews
  • Macron Loses Absolute Majority in Parliament as Opposition Surges
  • Francia Márquez Has Just Become Colombia’s First Black Vice President
  • A Herd in Exile: Riding Horses on Mozambique’s Bazaruto Archipelago
  • Your Monday Briefing
  • Ukraine Bans Some Russian Music and Books
  • Before He Ran for President, Gustavo Petro Was a Guerrilla Fighter
  • Paul Haggis Arrested on Sexual Assault Charges in Italy
  • Your Monday Briefing: Russia Makes Slow Gains in the Donbas Region
  • Russian Forces Tighten Noose Around Important Cities in Ukraine’s East
  • Children of Ukraine’s Fallen Soldiers Spend Father’s Day in New Grief
  • Ukraine rushes troops to reinforce its faltering defense of Sievierodonetsk.
  • FINA Restricts Transgender Women From Competing at Elite Level
  • France’s Far Right Wins Record Number of Seats in Parliament
  • Establishment conservatives quickly aligned with Hernández after his unexpected second-place win.
  • Germany Says It Will Restart Coal Power Plants to Save Natural Gas
  • Arizona Wildfire Destroys Observatory Buildings
  • Apple Workers at Maryland Store Vote to Unionize, a First in the U.S.
  • Parts of Yellowstone Will Reopen This Week After Damaging Floods
  • In Uvalde, the Healing Power of a Perfect Pitch
  • 15-Year-Old Is Killed and 3 Are Shot in Gunfire at D.C. Music Festival
  • Heat Wave to Shift East After Posing Dangerous Conditions in the Plains
  • Why a Rhodes Scholar’s Ambition Led Her to a Job at Starbucks
  • French Nuclear Power Crisis Frustrates Europe’s Push to Quit Russian Energy
  • China’s Once-Sizzling Property Market Has Started to Cool
  • When Customers Say Their Money Was Stolen on Zelle, Banks Often Refuse to Pay
  • ‘Lightyear’ Opens at No. 2, as ‘Jurassic World’ Holds Strong
  • Tracking the Fear Index on Wall Street
  • How Can You Travel for $100 or Less a Day? Get a Cruise Deal.
  • Google Says It’s Time for Longtime Small-Business Users to Pay Up
  • How a Religious Sect Landed Google in a Lawsuit
News from the New York Times

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 20 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Intel seeks $624 million in interest from EU after antitrust fine win
  • India calls off hundreds of trains as more protests loom over recruitment
  • Canada to ban making, importing many single-use plastics from December
  • Morgan Stanley sees stronger yuan on possible US trade tariff relief
  • Rybus dropped from Poland squad after joining Russian club
  • Korean Air CEO concerned over rising interest rates, inflation and competition
  • Explainer-How will swimming's new transgender rules work?
  • 'We will stay there, we won't sell': Residents unaffected by new SERS criteria requiring 5 years minimum occupancy before resale
  • Meta loses appeal in Russian court over 'extremist activity' tag -TASS
  • Germany sticks to 2030 coal exit target despite energy crisis
  • Alpine skiing-Swiss, Austrians in quartet appealing FIS election result to CAS
  • Tencent forms 'extended reality' unit as metaverse race gathers steam - sources
  • Delta, Kellogg join IBM pledge to fight bias in online ad targeting
  • Malaysia MP clarifies comments on how floods can be opportunities to promote 'volunteer tourism'
  • Court delays Garuda Indonesia's debt deal ratification for a week
  • Greece to subsidise new fridges, air conditioners to cut energy bill
  • Singapore studying effectiveness and safety of Moderna, Pfizer-BioNTech’s COVID-19 vaccines for children 6 months and older

  • Japan court rules same-sex marriage ban is not unconstitutional
  • West Ham sign defender Aguerd from Rennes

  • 26 more dead in India monsoon fury, waters recede in Bangladesh
  • More than 165,000 daily crossings at Singapore-Malaysia land borders since Apr 1: Johor government

  • North Korea abruptly stops importing COVID-19 containment goods from China
  • China's COVID-19 mass testing mantra is building a waste mountain
  • China's May oil imports from Russia soar 55% to a record, surpass Saudi supply
  • Macao shuts most businesses, restaurants amid mass COVID-19 testing; casinos stay open
  • Taiwan rattled by 6.0 magnitude quake, no immediate damage reported
  • Commentary: The US ‘pivot to Asia’ is back, but will it be enough?
  • China issues notice on supporting cross border yuan settlement for foreign trade
  • Fifteen EU nations say bloc must urgently accelerate trade deals
  • ABB blames 'challenging' market for delaying e-charging IPO again
  • Chinese splash out on tech goods, camping gear in shopping fest
  • Head of airlines group IATA confident industry will return to profitability in 2023
  • 'Huge uncertainty' for EU firms over China's COVID-19 curbs, chamber warns
  • Pfizer to buy 8.1% stake in French vaccines company Valneva
  • BOJ and govt closely coordinating on FX, Kuroda says after meeting PM
  • China's Beidaihe, site of leadership conclave, bars Tesla cars for 2 months
  • Credit Suisse promotes APAC ECM syndicate bankers - memo
  • Crypto industry fears contagion as bitcoin slips under $20,000
  • Singapore allocated more places for Haj pilgrimage this year: MUIS
  • Civil servants to receive 0.35-month mid-year bonus; one-time payment for junior grade officers
  • Social media services to be directed to remove harmful online content under proposed codes of practice by Government
  • 5 things you may not know about the Cathay Building
  • Ticket collection for NDP 2022, preview shows to begin from Jul 8
  • 80,000 seniors have not taken their COVID-19 booster shots: Ong Ye Kung
  • StarHub extends Premier League package promo offer for second time until Jun 30
  • Swimming: Singapore's Teong Tzen Wei finishes 8th at FINA World Championships
  • The Big Read: The Sports Hub nightmare – what went wrong according to insiders, and can the Government run it better?
  • German FA boosts membership, confident of bounce back from COVID-19 pandemic
  • Inter will bring in Mkhitaryan and Onana, Lukaku and Dybala targets: Marotta
  • Rennie urges Australia not to quit Super Rugby in 2024
  • FINA official hopes other sports follow transgender ruling
  • Care and Billy Vunipola back in England squad for Australia
  • South Korea to bid to host Asian Cup
  • Chelsea chairman Buck to step down at end of June
  • Minister wants UK sports bodies to follow FINA's lead in transgender policy
  • Georgia nationality switch allows Russia-born Dzalamidze to play Wimbledon
  • India coach Dravid backs Pant despite poor South Africa series
  • Thomas ready to grab his chance at Tour de France
  • Australia's Seebohm backs FINA decision on transgender swimmers

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 20 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 20 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-20 14:23PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong’s iconic Jumbo Floating Restaurant capsizes in South China Sea after encountering adverse conditions
  • Travel disruption to spill into first quarter of 2023, head of global airline association warns amid labour shortage and rising demand
  • Hong Kong could start quarantine-free business travel corridor with mainland China, fight for more flexible rules on border reopening, advisers urge
  • 1 in 10 Hong Kong kindergartens warn of possible closure in coming years as enrolment declines
  • Hong Kong government to give eligible elderly receiving allowances an extra HK$995 a month from September 1
  • Hong Kong Bar Association head stresses importance of ‘public perception’ after incoming deputy justice minister retains party membership
  • Debate over whether Hong Kong was indeed British colony reflects lack of education on Chinese constitution, lawmakers say
  • Hong Kong film star Louis Koo sees G Storm poster image misappropriated by Chinese NFT platform
  • ‘Spicy policies’ helped fortify Hong Kong’s market, save homeowners from negative-equity trap, defaults, ex-HKMA CEO Chan says
  • Coronavirus: hotel quarantine for top Hong Kong officials, advisers and lawmakers cut to 1 day ahead of July 1 celebrations; city logs 1,186 local cases
  • Hong Kong’s interbank rate climbs to two-year high as HKMA’s intervention saps liquidity from city’s currency market
  • Wealth gap between Hong Kong’s crazy rich, miserably poor widens since handover
  • Hong Kong’s environmental problems, from plastic pollution to filthy air to food waste, and the people fighting them
  • Beijing wants this Guangzhou district to become young Hongkongers ‘new home’, but what will make it work?
  • ‘Rusty’ aviation industry needs time to get flowing again, with passengers facing higher ticket prices amid rising fuel costs and staff shortages, Qantas chief warns
  • Inspiring memories of Harrow International’s first decade
  • Beijing underlines its jurisdiction and desire for urgent action by laying down targets for incoming Hong Kong leader, analysts say
  • HKEX to offer more A-share derivatives and financial products to affirm Hong Kong’s role as China’s offshore capital hub, Aguzin says
  • Coronavirus: elderly Hongkongers turn to expressive arts therapy to ease pandemic blues
  • Mass evacuations as record rain, floods strike southern China
  • Cop 15: UN Biodiversity Conference set to move from China to Canada after Covid-19 delays
  • China’s anti-corruption drive brings tighter rules for officials’ families to stop them exploiting their connections
  • China’s Russian oil imports surged over 50 per cent in May, boosted by discounts amid Ukraine war sanctions
  • China says latest anti-ballistic missile test ‘achieved objective’
  • US and Chinese businesses should team up to tackle climate change, says former US treasury secretary Paulson
  • What’s the key to stopping Covid overload of health systems in China? Focus on the vulnerable, study finds
  • Tencent news aggregator app Kuai Bao to cease operations next month, as China’s Big Tech firms cut noncore businesses
  • Chinese police make arrests in Henan banking scandal that has put spotlight on financial risks
  • China’s Covid mass testing regime is creating a sea of medical waste
  • China’s tattoo ban for minors criticised as circular logic and irrational fear of difference, but many welcome new rules
  • Taiwan’s Thunderbolt-2000 rocket system explodes during live-combat training
  • China’s coronavirus controls top concern for European firms, who may ‘vote with their feet’ if uncertainty persists
  • European students on the brink, locked out by China’s zero-Covid rules
  • NetEase delays highly anticipated release of Diablo Immortal in big setback for China’s No. 2 gaming company amid ongoing Weibo ban
  • Earthquake rattles Taiwan, no immediate damage reported
  • China resists loan rate cuts, but changes ‘still likely’ as economy struggles under zero-Covid
  • ‘Mind-reading’ device to detect porn could speed China’s policing of illicit content, say researchers
  • Rights and wrongs of US-China dispute over the Taiwan Strait
  • Next US election may prove most dangerous time for Taiwan, warns veteran US diplomat Chas Freeman
  • How the US’ Xinjiang labour law has crippled China’s cotton industry before even entering effect
  • Japan court upholds gay marriage ban, saying it is constitutional
  • Rice job: Philippines’ Bongbong Marcos appoints himself agriculture chief, plans ‘impossible’ rice price slash
  • Airlines restoring flights faster than planned to meet demand
  • Should Philippines’ Marcos be ‘on guard’ as Sara Duterte is sworn in early, meets China envoy?
  • Indonesia looks to Singapore to offload its surplus chickens
  • Fears for judo’s future in Japan as bullied, burnt-out children quit in droves
  • Three generations of Filipino-New Zealand family killed in ‘shocking’ crash
  • Filipino lawyer shot dead while on US holiday with mother: ‘I will bring him home soon in a box’
  • Australian PM refuses to demand US drop prosecution of Julian Assange
  • Burger King Japan swaps French fries for ramen after running low on potatoes
  • Will Malaysia see a fresh influx of Rohingya refugees as life in Bangladesh, Myanmar gets worse?
  • Russian journalist sells Nobel Prize for Ukrainian children – worth millions
  • Ukraine’s Zelensky expects Russia attacks to intensify as EU decision looms
  • Britain braces for biggest rail strike in decades
  • Colombia braces for change after ex-rebel Gustavo Petro wins presidency
  • Bitcoin climbs 7.6 per cent to pass US$20,400, signalling recovery from recent drop
  • EU seeks to release Ukrainian grain stuck due to Russia’s sea blockade
  • Ukraine war: ‘Fair trial’ urged for Moroccan fighter facing death penalty
  • World swimming’s governing body bans transgender athletes from women’s events
  • France’s Emmanuel Macron loses parliament majority in stunning setback
  • Oscar-winning director Paul Haggis detained in Italy over sexual assault claims
  • After British journalist’s murder in the Amazon, can his vision survive?
  • Israel’s foreign minister Yair Lapid to visit Türkiye amid security jitters
  • British MPs call for Wimbledon to drop HSBC as a sponsor over endorsement of Hong Kong’s national security law
  • US-China trade truce is needed to lift the global economic gloom
  • Hong Kong stocks advance as Chinese developers rally on easing bets while Macau casinos slide amid virus outbreak
  • How Japan’s foray into a digital yen could be its key to the Web 3.0 universe
  • Singapore office-design firm Space Matrix acquires B2B e-commerce site Pursuite amid strong demand for flexible workspaces
  • China-focused funds reload Alibaba, JD.com stocks to defy ‘uninvestable’ tag as Goldman’s worst-is-over view gains traction
  • Chinese game studio XD makes US a priority in its overseas expansion drive as home market becomes increasingly difficult to navigate
  • Chinese court rules car sale made using digital currency invalid as Beijing’s unrelenting crypto crackdown continues
  • How artificial intelligence will redefine the future of work
  • Science fiction is best placed to discuss climate crisis, says Chen Qiufan, Chinese sci-fi author
  • K-drama Jinxed at First: Seohyun, Na In-woo in cutesy fantasy romcom that comes on a little strong
  • Prince William’s style at 40: how his fashion choices have changed under Kate’s influence and the brands he now loves
  • Why retiring in Portugal for Hong Kong people has long been seen as a viable and welcoming option, today more than ever
  • What is threadfin? ‘Almost like salmon, but without the bad rep,’ say chefs of fish local to Hong Kong waters
  • Australia’s Aboriginal history celebrated in stunning new Sydney monument overlooking the opera house
  • Aphasia: what it is, how it happens and how to help those suffering from it and related conditions
  • Who are the richest Hunger Games stars? Net worths, ranked – from Oscar winner Jennifer Lawrence and OG actor Woody Harrelson, to rock legend Lenny Kravitz and hunky Hemsworth brother Liam
  • Who is Britney Spears’ new sister-in-law, Fay Asghari? The Iranian-American beauty is a nurse with her own aesthetic clinic in Beverly Hills, and even sat in front of Madonna at brother Sam’s wedding
  • Who is Lucy Guo, richest self-made woman under 40 after Kylie Jenner? The wealthy tech CEO co-founded Scale AI with world’s youngest billionaire Alexandr Wang – and is neighbours with David Beckham
  • Guido Terreni, CEO of Swiss watchmaker Parmigiani Fleurier on the women’s timepiece market, heading up a younger luxury brand, his China consumer strategy – and the Tonda PF GMT Rattrapante
  • Radhika Merchant’s big dance debut! Mukesh and Nita Ambani’s future daughter-in-law dazzled celebrities from Salman Khan to Ranveer Singh in her arangetram, hosted by Anant and family
  • 18 celebrities who came out as LGBT in 2022, so far: from Rebel Wilson and Selling Sunset’s Chrishell Stause to music stars Janelle Monáe and Emeli Sandé, and RuPaul’s Drag Race queens Bosco and Willow Pill
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Financial Times: 20 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Europe does not face fresh sovereign debt crisis, says eurogroup chief
  • Fed’s Mester warns returning inflation to 2% will take ‘a couple of years’
  • The deafening silence over Brexit’s economic fallout
  • Farewell to Russia and to the Sinatra doctrine
  • China IPO fundraising doubles US total to top global ranks
  • Live news updates: Russia threatens retaliation against Lithuania over Kaliningrad restrictions
  • European shares rise after heavy weekly decline for global stocks
  • Macron loses majority in French assembly
  • Emmanuel Macron crashes to earth after losing his hold on parliament
  • Revlon bankruptcy complicated by Citi’s $900mn error
  • Leftist Gustavo Petro wins Colombian presidency
  • Summer books of 2022: Economics
  • Activist shareholder Nelson Peltz in tussle with insurgent investors
  • China denies building naval bases but fear of its military reach grows
  • NetEase shares fall after nationalist backlash in China over Winnie the Pooh post
  • Celsius warns it will ‘take time’ to stabilise as bitcoin hovers near $20,000
  • Crypto industry braced for fallout after weekend meltdown
  • Paramount looks for its own ‘Squid Game’ to boost streaming expansion
  • Impasse over Russian blockade of Ukraine’s grain fuels global food crisis
  • Ukraine war hits global timber trade and adds to risks for forests
  • Pfizer buys stake in French vaccine maker Valneva
  • Long shadow of Russian money raises tricky questions for Swiss bankers
  • After the war ends, can Ethiopia’s economic ‘miracle’ get back on track?
  • Africa needs $25bn a year of investment to boost energy provision, says IEA chief
  • EasyJet cuts back summer schedule in effort to avoid further disruption
  • The new workplace: what young starters need to know
  • Private equity groups make £1.6bn takeover approach for Euromoney
  • Miners/iron ore: faltering Chinese demand highlights problems ahead
  • Business attacks ‘hugely short-sighted’ HS2 plans
  • The British should stop being so relaxed about the weak pound
  • TUC warns strikes will spread across UK as rail stoppage looms
  • China gaming/NetEase: Winnie the Pooh lapse is honey for the bears
  • Battling to define success after the WTO summit
  • No, Russia won’t replace Swift with the blockchain
  • Will boomers cause more inflation?
  • FirstFT: Colombia ushers in the most leftwing government in its history
  • EU companies say China units increasingly isolated from HQ by zero-Covid
  • Rail strike to test transport unions and inflation strategy
  • Bear market to the rescue
  • French voters deal blow to Macron agenda

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
